text.title: claude-code-hooks Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for claude-code-hooks as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Hook script ignores stdin — no INPUT=$(cat) at the start. The script exits immediately without consuming the stdin pipe.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: In some shell environments, an unconsumed stdin pipe causes the hook to block waiting for EOF that never arrives, hanging the Claude session until the hook timeout triggers. Always consume stdin as the first command, even if unused.

failure-mode.description: Hook returns invalid JSON on stdout (trailing newline, mixed text and JSON, missing closing brace). Claude Code surfaces a parse error in the conversation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Invalid JSON breaks the hook contract and can prevent the tool from executing (for PreToolUse) or produce session-visible errors (for PostToolUse). Validate hook JSON output with `jq .` during testing before adding to settings.json.

failure-mode.description: PreToolUse block action without a "reason" field: {"action":"block"}.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Claude interprets a block without a reason as a transient error and retries the tool call. The session enters a loop. Always include "reason" to terminate the retry and present the explanation to the user.

failure-mode.description: Directly modifying settings.json by appending JSON fragments without reading the existing structure first. Results in malformed JSON that breaks all hooks until manually repaired.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: settings.json is a single JSON object. Appending to it outside proper JSON structure produces a parse error on next Claude Code load, silently disabling all hooks. Use the update-config skill which merges safely.

failure-mode.description: Hook ordering: adding a linter hook before a formatter hook in the PostToolUse array. The linter sees unformatted code and reports violations that will be auto-fixed by the formatter.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Multiple hooks for the same event execute in array order. Formatters must run before linters; linters must run before tests. The array order in settings.json is the execution order.

failure-mode.description: PostToolUse hook without a matcher that runs on every tool call including Read and Glob, adding several seconds of latency to every file read.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: An unmatched PostToolUse hook runs on all tool calls. A formatter hook running on Read operations processes files that were not edited, wasting time and potentially reformatting files Claude is only reading for reference.
